TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_life_Kuatu

S.FORM: sua tia sua tia kari maku ia.
S.TRANSL: 我要說的是……
What I am going to talk is...

S.FORM: te ku poisua cani pininga maku miana.
S.TRANSL: 我要講我以前的家庭。
I am going to say about my previous family.

S.FORM: aranai ku miana er mecocua ma er 'arupara mataa 'Angai.
S.TRANSL: 我從以前，跟Angai結婚。
I got married with 'Angai in the past.

S.FORM: 'akia cu mamarangin 'Angai mecocua kim mamia.
S.TRANSL: 'Angai的父母過世了；只有我們兩個在一起。
'Angai's parents had died; only we two were together.

S.FORM: er arane miana miaranasʉ kia mo'uma.
S.TRANSL: 從以前開始，我(們)就很困苦的作農。
Since the past, I (we) have been tough transplanting seedlings.

S.FORM: u no mia 'una ci kia manu ia miaranasʉ kia putukikio sii nuu ca'ania ia tumatang manu.
S.TRANSL: 當我有了孩子的時候，我很困苦的工作，因為白天的時候小孩子在哭泣。
When I got the children, I was tough working, because the children cried during the daytime.

S.FORM: macici taniarʉ ia 'esi tumatang manu.
S.TRANSL: 太陽很熱，小孩子在哭泣。
The sun was hot and the children was crying.

S.FORM: nuu 'umo'ucanin ia miaranasʉ kia sii 'akia taru'an.
S.TRANSL: 如果下雨的話，我很困苦，因為沒有工寮。
If it rained I was tough, because there were no workmen's huts.

S.FORM: er makasua er miaranasʉ putukikio.
S.TRANSL: (我)那樣困苦的工作。
(I) was tough in that way.

S.FORM: er tatia cu manu arʉn cu cau.
S.TRANSL: 孩子長大了，別人娶走了。
The children grew up, and they were taken by others.

S.FORM: er 'akia manu maku sarunei kara kaira nannakʉ.
S.TRANSL: 我沒有男孩，都是女的。
I don't have boys; all are girls.

S.FORM: arʉn cu cau kangvang 'eci kim mamia mecocua mataa rʉ'ʉvʉ maku.
S.TRANSL: 所有(小孩)都被人娶走了，就只有和我的伴侶，我們兩個人在一起。
(My children) were all taken away by others, we two were together, only (I) and my mate.

S.FORM: er makasua marang cu kia.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣，我老了。
In that way, I turned old.

S.FORM: miaranasʉ putukikio 'akia moran sii niarʉ cu cau kangvang maman maku.
S.TRANSL: 我很困苦的工作，沒有(人)幫(我)，因為我的小孩全部被娶走了。
I was tough working, and no (one) helped (me), because my kids had all been taken by others.

S.FORM: 'apanapen 'apanapa nuu tia matapari'i manu sii avʉʉn avʉʉn na kukuca.
S.TRANSL: 因為是背在後面，有時候小孩會掉下來。
Because (the children) were carried on the back, sometimes the children would fall down.

S.FORM: ah aka cʉpʉng te ci kia manan putukikio sii makahua manu um 'esi tumatang.
S.TRANSL: 因為小孩子那樣哭著，(我)心裡很難過；我要怎麼工作?
Because the children were crying in such a way, I felt sad; how could I work?

S.FORM: er makahua kia miaranasʉ aratatia cu manu mama'ari cu um matarava mamisa masin.
S.TRANSL: 我那樣困苦著。小孩變大了，到了現在，(狀況)就稍好了。
I was tough in that way. The children have grown up; at the moment (the condition) is better.

S.FORM: er 'eci kia mamia mecocua mataa rʉ'ʉvʉ sii .
nuu matarava nuu kaisis in cina ia iavatu tanasa maman ara.
S.TRANSL: 若到了媽媽的節日，小孩就來到家。
When Mother's Day came, the children came home.

S.FORM: um movua nupasʉ tia 'urupaca mu cina cuma sii 'an ci kim tavarʉ'ʉ mon kamua.
S.TRANSL: 「(這些錢)是要給媽媽爸爸你們使用的金錢，因為我們不能住你們那裡。
"(We) give money to you mother and father to use, because we cannot live in your place.

S.FORM: sii 'ekia pipining kavanvantuku.
S.TRANSL: 因為我們在外面賺錢。
Because we are earning money outside.

S.FORM: er makasua cu mama'ari cu cʉpʉng nuu makasu in.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣了。如果那樣的話，心裡就舒坦了。
The condition was like so. In that way, my heart would be relieved.

S.FORM: marang ci kia paira cu mamia musukom.
S.TRANSL: 我老了，只會經常生病。
I am already old (and) can only get sick often.

S.FORM: aka cu tina'an 'an ci kia tavarʉ'ʉ putukikio.
S.TRANSL: 身體不好了，我不能工作了。
(Because) my body is not well, I cannot work.

S.FORM: makasua cu mamia aranai mia tatingi kia makasi cu mamarurang ci kia.
S.TRANSL: 只有那樣了。從我年紀小，就這樣一直到我老了。
Simply in that way. I am aged from the time of youth this way.

S.FORM: aka cʉpʉng sii 'an ci kia tavarʉ'ʉ putukikio.
S.TRANSL: 心裡很難過，因為我不能工作了。
I feel sad because I cannot work.

S.FORM: er nʉkai mamisa ia mama'ari cu sii u su cʉngfu ia vʉʉn ci kia mamarurang um nupasʉ tia 'urupacaa mamarurang mama'ari cu cʉpʉng nuu pacʉpʉcʉpʉng.
S.TRANSL: 但是最近呢，好一點了，因為政府給我(們)老人家，老人家要使用的金錢，若想起來，心裡就舒坦了。
But recently, the condition is getting better, because the government gives us the elderly money, which we can use; when I think of that, my heart gets relieved.
